description: Burchardia umbellata, known as milkmaids, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to woodlands and heath of eastern and southern Australia. It is known in all states. It typically flowers from September until November, in dry sclerophyll forests.
origin: SOA, AUSTRALASIA, NSW, TAS OO, VIC OO
synonyms: ["Burchardia umbellata var. typica", "Burchardia umbellata var. parviflora", "Burchardia umbellata f. minor", "Reya umbellata", "Burchardia rigida"]

care.tips: Native to: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, QLD QU, VIC OO

description: Colchicum alpinum, the alpine autumn crocus, is a corm-forming perennial with pale, delicate rosy-purple flowers, similar to C. autumnale but smaller. It is native to the Alps and the Appennini of Italy, Switzerland, France and Sicily, and cultivated as an ornamental in other regions.
origin: ITA VC, SIC, SWI, SWI OO, FRA CI
synonyms: ["Colchicum alpinum subsp. parvulum", "Colchicum alpinum proles merenderoides", "Colchicum vallis-demonis", "Colchicum alpinum var. merenderoides", "Colchicum merenderoides", "Colchicum alpinum var. parvulum", "Colchicum pseudoparvulum", "Colchicum parvulum"]

care.tips: Native to: MIDDLE EUROPE, SIC, ITA SM, ITA VC, FRA | Similar plants: Dianthus chinensis x barbatus
care.toxicity_note: Human: very strong toxic. Parts: Whole plant, highest concentrations in bulb, seed. (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)
care.toxic_parts: Whole plant, highest concentrations in bulb, seed
care.edible_parts: Highly toxic and possibly fatal if ingested.

description: Colchicum autumnale, commonly known as autumn crocus, meadow saffron, naked boys or naked ladies, is a toxic autumn-blooming flowering plant that resembles the true crocuses, but is a member of the plant family Colchicaceae, unlike the true crocuses, which belong to the family Iridaceae. It is called "naked boys/ladies" because the flowers emerge from the ground long before the leaves appear.
synonyms: ["Colchicum drenowskii", "Colchicum autumnale var. bulgaricum", "Colchicum autumnale var. pictum", "Colchicum borisii", "Bulbocodium antumnale", "Colchicum commune", "Colchicum polyanthon", "Colchicum autumnale f. macropetala", "Colchicum autumnale var. transsilvanicum", "Colchicum rhodopaeum"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: temperate
care.tips: Similar plants: Lantana camara | Native to: BGM BE, IRE NI, FRA FR, UKR MO, SPA GI
care.toxicity_note: Toxic to Dogs, Toxic to Cats, Toxic to Horses. Signs: Bloody vomiting, diarrhea, shock, multi-organ damage, bone marrow suppression
care.toxic_parts: Whole plant, highest concentrations in bulb, seed
care.toxicity_symptoms: Bloody vomiting, diarrhea, shock, multi-organ damage, bone marrow suppression
care.toxicity_first_aid: Remove any remaining plant material from mouth. Rinse with water. Monitor for symptoms (vomiting, drooling, lethargy). Call poison control or vet if symptoms appear.
care.used_for: ["Despite its poisonous reputation", "it is sometimes used in the treatment of gout."]
care.used_for_details: Despite its poisonous reputation, it is sometimes used in the treatment of gout.
care.edible_parts: Highly toxic and can be fatal if ingested.

description: Naked flower stems rise from the ground to 4 to 6 inches tall in late summer to early fall bearing goblet-shaped flowers (to 3 inches long).

description: Disporum uniflorum, commonly known as yellow fairy bells, is a species of perennial herb in the genus Disporum. It is native to Korea.
origin: CHN TJ, CHC CQ, CHS MA, CHM HJ, CHN GS
synonyms: ["Disporum sessile subsp. flavens", "Disporum sessile var. flavens", "Disporum flavens", "Disporum sessile var. pachyrrhizum"]

description: Disporum viridescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Colchicaceae. Like other species in the genus, it grows from a rhizome. The plant is 30 to 80 cm, with a stem that may branch. The leaves are more-or-less ovate in shape with a very short petiole (stalk). One or two open flowers are borne at the ends of stems; they have greenish white tepals which are 1.5 to 2 cm long. Flowering is in late spring to early summer. Black berries around 1 cm (0.
Gloriosa superba is a species of flowering plant in the family Colchicaceae. Common names include flame lily, climbing lily, creeping lily, glory lily, gloriosa lily, tiger claw, and fire lily.
synonyms: ["Gloriosa cirrhifolia", "Gloriosa rockefelleriana", "Gloriosa rothschildiana", "Gloriosa superba f. angustifolia", "Gloriosa nepalensis", "Gloriosa verschuurii", "Methonica senegalensis", "Gloriosa doniana", "Eugone superba", "Gloriosa lutea"]

description: 3-celled seed capsule with 3 rounded lobes and a rounded tip. The cells contain several seeds. They display from July to August

care.light_guide: NC State: Deep shade (Less than 2 hours to no direct sunlight)

description: The fruit displays from June to August.  Each three sided capsule will split along 2 or more seams, releasing 2 or more seeds.

care.light_guide: NC State: Deep shade (Less than 2 hours to no direct sunlight)
care.edible_parts: The leaves and roots are edible.

description: The fruit is a ¾ to 1¼ inch long capsule, generally oval to round with an abrupt point at the tip and tapering at the base to a short stalk. There are 3 distinct winged edges around the capsule.

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)
care.edible_parts: Young shoots are said to have a flavor reminiscent of asparagus. Tea made from the roots has been used to treat diarrhea and other ailments.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Aletris root is used in herbal medicine to treat stomach problems such as colic, constipation, diarrhea, gas, and upset stomach.
